items.title: D2. True cross-application role-reframed study

items.title: D3. Insight-action gap heavy bridge follow-ups
items.verification: Either run a pre-registered Bridge 3b/4/5 follow-up with exports and paper-boundary text, or close the follow-up as too expensive for the expected marginal mechanism information.

items.title: D4. Architecture-matched SEL disposition-gradient replication
items.verification: Decide whether the cells 40-45 × SEL clean replication is worth the cost; if run, report the architecture-scope result, otherwise close as out-of-scope for the current paper.

items.title: D6. Orientation-family taxonomy extensions
items.verification: Decide whether cognitivist-only, pure Socratic, radical constructivist, culturally responsive, or Freirean variants are needed; if yes, add cells/prompts/tests and report results, otherwise close as deferred.

items.verification: Paper read; a one-line note records whether it changes our rubric, prompts, architecture or eval design; this item is then closed or spawns a concrete task.

items.title: Human-learner validation pilot (real learning gains)
items.verification: Phased N≈90 pilot runs end-to-end across 3 arms; pre/post MCQ + immediate transfer + coded free-text explanation + 1-week retention scored; the tutor-quality → learning-gains path is estimated (or shown null) in exports/.
items.blocked_by: IRB approval + real consent text + real item content (content/legal track, not engineering)

items.title: Scriptorium control-room front door and first-run data health
items.verification: The dashboard front door clearly identifies Scriptorium, shows role-based next actions, distinguishes DB-backed script corpus from file-backed proof runs, and gives a no-spend path out of an empty script corpus.

items.title: Scriptorium creation flows for compose, launch, and job monitoring
items.verification: Mock generation, mock derivation, replay dry-run, and score dry-run are launchable from guided UI flows with visible cost class, command preview, validation checklist, and job records.

items.title: Scriptorium responsive shell and navigation model
items.verification: Core Scriptorium and injected static routes have no horizontal overflow at 390x844 or 768x1024, the mobile shell uses a compact app bar/drawer, and route parity/browser tests still pass.

items.title: Scriptorium UX safety net and smoke report
items.verification: A no-spend Scriptorium UX smoke command checks core routes at desktop and mobile widths for HTTP render, console errors, horizontal overflow, unlabeled controls, duplicate landmarks, touch-target risks, and emits a compact report/screenshots.

items.title: Build the workplan executable layer (CLI, ingester, dashboard panel, test)
items.verification: scripts/workplan.js list/show/add/triage/set/validate/render/ingest all run green; tests/workplan.test.js passes under npm run test:hermetic; board.json renders a panel in the scriptorium (:3466); npm run wp:* wired in package.json.

items.title: G5. § H2 per-session slope p=0.032 is a wrong p-value
items.verification: The per-session slope p-value is recomputed correctly; the paper text is fixed and provable-discourse re-checks the claim.

items.title: Scriptorium evidence workbenches for scripts, proof runs, and replays
items.verification: A reviewer can find, inspect, compare, flag, and label scripts/proof runs/replays from first-class evidence workbench views without route guessing.

items.title: Scriptorium P2 best-of-breed cockpit features
items.verification: Command palette, job-center links, evidence graph, comparison workbench permalinks, and saved-view restoration pass targeted tests plus the Scriptorium UX smoke.

items.title: Scriptorium review queue and workplan loop
items.verification: A flagged case can be found, reviewed, connected to a workplan item, and resolved through the existing workplan source-of-truth workflow without creating a second board.

items.title: Scriptorium static-tool and desktop quality pass
items.verification: Chat, adjudication, pilot-admin, and Electron desktop parity pass the same shell, responsive, accessibility, and no-console gates as the server-rendered Scriptorium routes.

items.title: Auto-drop daily-routine actions into the workplan inbox
items.verification: .github/workflows/workplan-ingest.yml runs `wp:ingest --daily` on a research-roundup PR and commits per-paper captures (deduped by arXiv id) to the PR branch; ingestDaily parses <div class="paper"> entries; the historical backfill produced 40 captures locally; workplan.js is lint-clean and wp:test is green.

items.title: Consolidate dialogue logs + DB into the private archive repo
items.verification: Dialogue logs are co-located with the DB under the private archive; npm run provenance:validate finds the logs; the dramatic fork's sibling-path log_dir stopgap is removed.

items.title: D1. Mechanistic feature OLS and multi-turn replication
items.verification: Reran D1 Pass 6 OLS, Pass 7 multi-turn scope replication, and cross-judge checks; exports and notes/d1-paper-integration.md are present.

items.title: Exclude own papers from daily research roundup captures
items.verification: scripts/workplan.js ingest --daily skips arXiv 2603.10450 via a regression fixture; npm run wp:test and npm run wp:check pass.

items.title: G2. §6.4 recognition effect sizes — reproducible once the column/judge/run are stated
items.verification: TODO's G-arc status records G2 closed; the paper now names the tutor_overall_score/Sonnet/run-bound basis for the effect-size claim and the optional analyzer is not a live blocker.

items.title: Geist in the Machine: Simulating Recognition and Inner Dialogue in AI-Mediated Teaching and Research
items.verification: Confirmed arXiv 2603.10450 is authored by Liam Magee; Paper 2.0 already cites the companion study via @magee2026geist, so no manuscript edit is required.

items.title: Workplan governance checks and GitHub mirror
items.verification: `npm run wp:check`, `npm run wp:test`, GitHub mirror dry-run, and `/board` Scriptorium smoke all passed before the branch was merged to main.

items.title: A18.38 Teacher-as-learner ML/fine-tuning ladder
items.verification: Captured as not greenlit; no weight-update or further widening work proceeds unless a new trusted signal changes the A18/A15 decision basis and a fresh item is opened.

items.title: B7. Split structural-critic peripeteia rule into earned vs named reorientation
items.verification: Dropped because the 2026-05-28 prompt-side surgery made earned and named reorientation co-occur reliably; no critic split is needed unless that evidence changes.
